action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 7 region-balanced 13-epoch model gives test worst-region 0.4659 (best). Iter 6 8-epoch no-balance gave 0.4566. Improvement is small. ~8.25h remaining, 22 iterations. Africa is consistent worst region. Analysis: WiSE-FT (linear interpolation of fine-tuned weights with zero-shot/initial weights) is known to substantially improve OOD on FMoW. Since I have CLIP-L/14 fine-tuned, I can implement WiSE-FT efficiently by mixing the fine-tuned vision encoder with the original. But class head was trained from scratch, so only the visual backbone can be interpolated. Alternative: simpler — try weight ensembling (SWA) of multiple checkpoints. Or train more epochs. Given time, let me implement WiSE-FT-style mixing: load initial CLIP visual + final fine-tuned visual, sweep alpha on val. Decision: First do WiSE-FT sweep with current best ckpt — minimal time cost. Save current model state with original CLIP weights too.
